raw_transcript: That's a classic description of 'biliary colic.' When you eat fat, your gallbladder squeezes to release bile. If you have gallstones, one of those stones can get stuck in the neck of the gallbladder while it's squeezing, causing that intense pressure.
final_corrected_note: That's a classic description of 'biliary colic.' When you eat fat, your gallbladder squeezes to release bile. If you have gallstones, one of those stones can get stuck in the neck of the gallbladder while it's squeezing, causing that intense pressure.
